title: Little Red Riding Hood
lines: Once upon a time there was a sweet little girl who was loved by everyone who knew her, but her grandmother loved her most of all.
The grandmother had made her a little red velvet hood that the girl loved so much she wore it all the time.
Everyone called her Little Red Riding Hood.
One day her mother said, 'Your grandmother is ill and weak; go to her house and take her this cake and butter.'
Little Red Riding Hood set out through the forest with her basket.
Before long she met a wolf, but she did not know how wicked he was.
'Where are you going, little girl?' asked the wolf.
'To my grandmother's house,' she replied, 'which lies beyond the mill.'
The wolf thought to himself that she would make a good meal, but he had a better plan.
'You should pick some flowers for your grandmother,' he suggested.
While Little Red Riding Hood was picking flowers, the wolf ran ahead to the grandmother's house.
He knocked on the door, and when the grandmother opened it, he swallowed her whole.
He put on her nightgown and cap and lay down in her bed.
When Little Red Riding Hood arrived at the house, something seemed strange.
'Grandmother, what big ears you have!' she said.
'All the better to hear you with, my dear,' replied the wolf.
'Grandmother, what big eyes you have!'
'All the better to see you with, my dear.'
'Grandmother, what big teeth you have!'
'All the better to eat you with!' cried the wolf, and he jumped from the bed.
Just then a huntsman burst through the door and shot the wolf.
The huntsman cut the wolf open, and the grandmother sprang out alive and unhurt.
Little Red Riding Hood promised never to talk to strangers again.
